name: tamazia-audit-engine reference set
description: Ground truth for the reference-set harness. Engine output must MATCH or ABSTAIN on every verified expectation and must NEVER CONTRADICT one. Fields are filled only where a hand-verified source states them; everything else is null with needs_verification: true. Do not fill a null field without citing a verification source in provenance.
sources: Tamazia-Remix/AUDIT-ENGINE-FAILURE-CATALOGUE.md (nine forensic audits hand-verified against live sites, 2026-06-23)

semantics.jurisdiction_codes: UK, EU, US, AE, IE. sub-jurisdiction noted in notes where known (e.g. US/Florida).
semantics.jurisdictions_bound: Where the firm has legal nexus (register, establishment, authorisation). The engine asserting a bound jurisdiction NOT in this list is a contradiction. The engine omitting one that IS in the list is an abstention (allowed, logged).
semantics.jurisdictions_serves: Marketing reach only. Serves never attaches law by itself.
semantics.expected_frameworks_min: Frameworks that verifiably bind this firm. Missing = abstention (allowed, logged). These are names, matched loosely; catalogue ids are attached in P2.
semantics.known_breaches: Hand-verified real problems on the live site. Engine finding them = pass; not finding them = abstention.
semantics.known_non_breaches: Hand-verified false positives from the forensic audits. The engine asserting ANY of these as a breach or attaching the named framework is a CONTRADICTION and fails the harness.
semantics.roles: forensic = one of the 9 hand-verified forensic audits. watch = 10-domain watch list (membership only, no verified verdicts yet). matrix = 8-firm legal/health x UK/IE/US/AE design matrix (cell intent noted, mostly unverified).

expected_frameworks_min: MHRA Human Medicines Regulations 2012 reg 280 (POM advertising)

known_breaches.description: GDPR breach verified real in the forensic audit.

known_non_breaches.description: Modern Slavery statement duty does not apply: turnover below the 36m GBP threshold (failure class C1). Attaching MSA or reporting a missing statement is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Original engine sector tag was 'General' (wrong, class A2); the whole aesthetics pack was missed (class D1, D2). Headline conflated critical count with breached-framework count (F2). legal_name and company_number not stated in the source; verify via Companies House before filling.

known_breaches.description: Broken /privacy-policy link on the live site (failure class G3).

known_non_breaches.description: DIFC does not apply; correctly screened out in the verified audit. Attaching DIFC is a contradiction.
ADGM does not apply; correctly screened out in the verified audit. Attaching ADGM is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Verdict: clean audit, correct jurisdiction (mainland UAE: PDPL + DHA). Displayed DHA licence number must be credited as a positive signal (class G2), not ignored. Minor severity nits only.

known_non_breaches.description: US (Miami, Florida) firm was catastrophically assessed entirely under UK law (failure class A1). Attaching ANY UK framework is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Sub-jurisdiction: Florida. Correct US framework list not yet hand-verified; leave expected_frameworks_min null until verified against FTC/state bar advertising rules.

expected_frameworks_min: GMC Good Medical Practice 2024 (advertising and social media)

known_non_breaches.description: Sole trader: Companies Act trading-disclosure duty mis-applied, wrong limb (failure class C3). Asserting a s.82 breach is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Sector/jurisdiction tags were correct. Other verified defects: 2025-08-16 archive data sold as 'live' (B4 - snapshot language must derive from the snapshot field); severity inflation on CMA/Equality items (E4); GMC missed (D2). Practitioner: Dr Tim Clayton, sole trader per the forensic audit.

known_breaches.description: Broken anchor on a policy link, verified on the live site (failure class G3).

known_non_breaches.description: Privacy policy IS present (footer-linked); 'missing privacy policy' was a crawler-coverage false positive (class B2/B3).
Cookie policy IS present (footer-linked); 'missing cookie policy' was a false positive (class B2/B3).
Modern Slavery statement IS present in the footer; 'missing MSA statement' was a false positive (class B2/B3).
Company number IS displayed in the footer; 'missing company number' was a false positive (class B3/C3).

firms.notes: Scoping was correct. Exposure was reported as $5.7M USD for a UK firm - currency must follow the binding jurisdiction (class E3, GBP here).

expected_frameworks_min: ICO Children's Code (Age Appropriate Design Code)

known_non_breaches.description: OfS is the higher-education regulator; it does not regulate a state school (failure class A3, school != university). Attaching OfS is a contradiction.
Consumer law applied without a B2C commerce context (failure class C2). Attaching CMA/Consumer Rights findings is a contradiction.
Company-law trading disclosures mis-applied to a free state school.
Modern Slavery statement duty does not apply (class C1).

firms.notes: Headline used the 18m GBP GDPR statutory cap as exposure (class E1) - exposure must come from realistic enforcement bands, and ICO posture on public-sector/education is reprimand-first (class E4).

known_non_breaches.description: B2B firm: consumer law applied without any B2C evidence (failure class C2). Contradiction.
Modern Slavery statement duty does not apply (class C1).
Wrong instrument within the regulator: MAR cited where financial-promotion rules were the relevant regime (class C5). Asserting a MAR breach is a contradiction.

known_non_breaches.framework: ICO Cookies Guidance (as a separate framework from PECR)
known_non_breaches.description: PECR and 'ICO Cookies Guidance' double-counted as two frameworks for one statute (class C4). Both attaching is a contradiction.
SPA was never JS-rendered: the 22 cookie findings were unverifiable (class B1). Any cookie finding asserted as breach (rather than needs-review) without rendered-DOM evidence is a contradiction.

firms.notes: 16m GBP headline was the GDPR cap applied to a PECR matter - PECR cap is 500k GBP (class E2). The audit claimed jurisdictions 'UK and US' while attaching zero US frameworks (class F4): the engine must not name US for this firm without evidence.

expected_frameworks_min: RERA/DLD Trakheesi permit and ORN/BRN display (Dubai real-estate marketing)

known_non_breaches.description: DIFC applied by keyword from service pages, not by establishment (class A4) - wrong. Attaching DIFC is a contradiction.
ADGM applied by keyword, not by establishment (class A4) - wrong. Attaching ADGM is a contradiction.
Cookie banner IS present (Complianz) but was reported missing because the SPA was not rendered (class B1). Asserting a missing cookie banner is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Verified in the forensic audit: EU + UK GDPR attachments were CORRECT (extraterritorial reach); federal PDPL was missed (class D6); RERA/Trakheesi was missed (class D5, Dubai real-estate activity). jurisdictions_serves reflects the verified GDPR reach; treat UK/EU GDPR as binding via that nexus while establishment stays AE mainland.

expected_frameworks_min: National Trading Standards material information (Parts A/B/C)

known_breaches.description: Thin privacy policy - GDPR finding verified real.

known_non_breaches.description: Company number IS disclosed; the Companies Act finding was a false positive (class B3/C3).
Modern Slavery statement duty does not apply (class C1).
Fees information IS present in footer-linked documents; the fees false positive came from unread PDFs (class B2).

firms.notes: Sector tag was correct but the entire property pack was missed (class D4) while an F grade was issued to a visibly compliant firm - TPO/ARLA/NAEA/CMP/AML badges must be credited (class G1).

known_non_breaches.framework: Another US state's bar advertising rules (NY / CA / FL / IL)
known_non_breaches.description: Munsch Hardt is established and admitted in Texas only (Austin, Dallas, Fort Worth, Houston). US state bar advertising rules are node-exclusive: each requires establishment/admission in that state (required_nexus established_in). Attaching the New York (NY_RPC_7_1 / NY_RPC_7_3_7_4), California (CA_RPC_CH7 / CA_BPC_6157), Florida (FL_BAR_4_7_CONTENT / FL_BAR_4_7_19) or Illinois (IL_RPC_7) bar rules to a Texas-only firm is the wrong-node contradiction (the SRA-vs-BSB class, caution C-052). Texas TDRPC Part VII is the correct instrument.

The firm is Texas-established with no California office. CCPA attaches only on a California nexus at threshold: doing business in California AND over 26,625,000 USD revenue, or 100,000+ CA consumers/households, or 50%+ revenue from selling data (US_CCPA_CPRA excluded_when). There is no evidence of a California establishment or CA-resident processing at threshold, and a law firm's website does not sell personal data. Attaching CCPA here is the blanket US-state-privacy false positive (caution C-055). Exact figures are needs_verification.

known_non_breaches.framework: Any UK or other foreign framework
known_non_breaches.description: A US (Texas) firm assessed under UK law is the immigrationlawyersusa catastrophe (caution C-006, class A1). Attaching UK GDPR, PECR, the SRA, the Companies Act or the Modern Slavery Act to this firm is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Watch-list member scoped with verified cell facts (following the russell-cooke matrix pattern of adding verified sector/jurisdiction to a non-forensic entry). Verified legal name from the munsch.com footer copyright: 'Munsch Hardt Kopf & Harr, P.C.', a Texas professional corporation; offices Austin, Dallas, Fort Worth and Houston (Texas only), so the sub_jurisdiction is TX. Cell: us-legal. legal_name and company_number are left null per the set convention (exact-match ground truth deferred; verify the P.C. registration via the Texas SoS / Comptroller and admissions via the State Bar of Texas before filling). expected_frameworks_min uses compiled catalogue ids (us-legal + us-universal). US_TDPSA is included because a firm of this size is not an SBA small business and processes Texas residents' personal data, so the small-business gate does not exempt it (needs_verification on the SBA determination). TX_TDRPC_7_04 (mandatory advertisement filing) is deliberately NOT in the minimum set: a firm's own website content is largely exempt from filing under Rule 7.05. US_ADA_TITLE_III_WEB carries a genuine circuit split (us-universal excluded_when), so it is an advisory minimum, not a certainty.
firms.provenance: Official firm site https://www.munsch.com/ (legal name in footer copyright '© 2026 Munsch Hardt Kopf & Harr, P.C.', Texas office locations, law-firm identity) fetched via WebFetch 2026-07-18; framework ids cross-checked against catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json (us-legal + us-universal cells).

known_non_breaches.description: Cedars-Sinai is a California NONPROFIT hospital. CCPA applies only to a for-profit 'business', so a nonprofit is outside its definition; and CCPA/state-privacy law exempts HIPAA protected health information and HIPAA-covered entities (US_CCPA_CPRA excluded_when: exempts data/entities already covered by HIPAA). A hospital's patient data is HIPAA PHI. Asserting a CCPA/CPRA breach is a contradiction on both the nonprofit and the HIPAA-PHI grounds. The exact for-profit/nonprofit registration is needs_verification.

known_non_breaches.framework: New York or Florida state medical board advertising rules
known_non_breaches.description: Cedars-Sinai is licensed and located in California (Los Angeles). State medical board advertising rules are node-exclusive (required_nexus established_in that state). The Medical Board of California (US_MEDBOARD_ADV_CA) is the correct instrument; attaching the New York (US_MEDBOARD_ADV_NY) or Florida (US_MEDBOARD_ADV_FL) board rules to a California-only provider is a wrong-node contradiction.
match_any: Medical Board Advertising Rules - New York

known_non_breaches.framework: Any UK or other foreign framework
known_non_breaches.description: A US (California) hospital assessed under UK law is the immigrationlawyersusa catastrophe (caution C-006, class A1). Attaching UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018, PECR or the Modern Slavery Act is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Watch-list member scoped with verified cell facts. Verified trading name 'Cedars-Sinai Medical Center' - a nonprofit academic medical center / teaching hospital in Los Angeles, California, and a HIPAA covered entity. The cedars-sinai.org header now also brands as 'Cedars-Sinai Health Sciences University', so the exact current legal entity name is left null and needs_verification (confirm via the California SoS / IRS Tax Exempt Organization Search before filling). Cell: us-healthcare. expected_frameworks_min uses compiled catalogue ids (us-healthcare + us-universal). US_HHS_504_WEB assumes the hospital receives HHS financial assistance (Medicare/Medicaid), which is near-certain for a hospital of this scale but marked needs_verification; US_CURES_INFOBLOCK applies because a healthcare provider is an actor under 45 CFR 171.102.
firms.provenance: Official site https://www.cedars-sinai.org/ ('A Nonprofit Hospital in Los Angeles') fetched via WebFetch 2026-07-18, corroborated by the public record (academic medical center, Los Angeles, California; HIPAA covered entity); framework ids cross-checked against catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json (us-healthcare + us-universal cells).

known_non_breaches.description: Kabod Coffee LLC is a small Denver specialty-coffee brand. The Colorado Privacy Act attaches only above a volume threshold: controlling/processing personal data of 100,000+ Colorado consumers, OR 25,000+ CO consumers while deriving revenue from selling personal data (US_CPA excluded_when). A small coffee roaster plausibly meets neither and does not sell personal data. Reporting a CPA breach is the threshold false positive (the Modern-Slavery-on-SMEs class, caution C-071 / C-055). Exact consumer counts are needs_verification.

known_non_breaches.framework: CCPA/CPRA and peer state privacy acts
known_non_breaches.description: The same small-scale threshold reasoning excludes CCPA/CPRA (26,625,000 USD revenue, or 100,000 CA consumers, or 50% data-sales - US_CCPA_CPRA excluded_when) and the peer state acts US_VCDPA, US_CTDPA and US_UCPA (each a 100,000-consumer / revenue threshold). A Denver coffee retailer below every applicability threshold is exempt; attaching them is the blanket US-state-privacy false positive (caution C-055).

known_non_breaches.framework: FTC Made in USA Labeling Rule (US_FTC_MADE_IN_USA)
known_non_breaches.description: Kabod makes no US-origin claim; it markets Ethiopian single-source beans, and 'roasted in Denver, Colorado' is a processing statement, not a product-origin 'Made in USA' claim. US_FTC_MADE_IN_USA excluded_when is 'no US-origin claims made'. Attaching it is a subject-trigger false positive (based on the pages fetched 2026-07-18).

known_non_breaches.framework: Any UK or other foreign framework
known_non_breaches.description: A US (Colorado) retailer assessed under UK law is the immigrationlawyersusa catastrophe (caution C-006, class A1). Attaching UK GDPR, PECR or the Modern Slavery Act is a contradiction.

firms.notes: Watch-list member scoped with verified cell facts, chosen as the us-universal cell exemplar (a general commercial e-commerce business whose relevant frameworks are the sector-agnostic us-universal pack). Verified from the site footer '© 2026 KABOD COFFEE LLC'; a Denver, Colorado specialty-coffee brand selling roasted beans direct-to-consumer via a Shopify store, with 100% organic / ethically sourced / fair-trade marketing claims and no reviews or subscriptions displayed on the pages fetched. sector is left null (the cell is universal, not a specific sector family) and legal_name/company_number null per the set convention. expected_frameworks_min: US_FTC_ACT_S5_UDAP over the marketing claims, US_FTC_GREEN_GUIDES for the sustainability/organic claims (advisory - applies to the environmental limb of those claims), and US_ADA_TITLE_III_WEB for the e-commerce site (which carries a circuit split). The known_non_breaches are the load-bearing threshold class: a small firm below the CCPA/CPA consumer and revenue thresholds. Note: Kabod recently opened a cafe in Ethiopia; the audited US web presence remains Colorado-established so jurisdictions_bound stays US.
firms.provenance: Official site https://kabodcoffee.com/ (footer '© 2026 KABOD COFFEE LLC'; Denver, Colorado; specialty coffee e-commerce; organic / ethically sourced / fair-trade claims; no reviews or subscriptions on the fetched pages) fetched via WebFetch 2026-07-18. Colorado SoS entity search (https://www.sos.state.co.us/) was attempted for corroboration but returned HTTP 403 (bot wall), so the Colorado registration is needs_verification. Framework ids cross-checked against catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json (us-universal cell).
